message:
fix(lab): record actual task outcome severity in replay trace

The replay recorder was hardcoded to record `Severity::Ok` for every task
completion, regardless of the actual outcome. This meant that cancelled or
errored tasks appeared as successful in replay traces, making it impossible
to reproduce severity-dependent scheduling decisions during deterministic
replay.

- Replace hardcoded `Severity::Ok` with `outcome.severity()` in
  `LabRuntime::poll_task_completion`
- Add regression test `replay_records_correct_severity_for_cancelled_task`
  that spawns a task returning `Outcome::Cancelled`, runs to quiescence,
  and verifies the replay trace contains `Severity::Cancelled` (2) rather
  than `Severity::Ok` (0)

Fixes bd-beuyd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
